relationship.description: A USER can place many ORDERs (ORDER.user_id references USER.user_id)
An ATTRACTION can be booked in many ORDERs (ORDER.attraction_id references ATTRACTION.attraction_id)
A USER can write many COMMENTs (COMMENT.user_id references USER.user_id)
An ATTRACTION can receive many COMMENTs (COMMENT.attraction_id references ATTRACTION.attraction_id)
A USER can send many MESSAGEs (MESSAGE.user_id references USER.user_id)
A USER can have many FAVORITE records linking to ATTRACTIONs (FAVORITE.user_id references USER.user_id)
An ATTRACTION can be favorited by many USERS (FAVORITE.attraction_id references ATTRACTION.attraction_id)

metadata.improvements_made: Added explicit primary key attributes (user_id, attraction_id, order_id, comment_id, message_id, favorite_id). Introduced foreign key attributes in ORDER, COMMENT, MESSAGE, and FAVORITE to enforce referential integrity. Included an 'is_active' flag for soft‑delete handling on all entities. Clarified enumerated fields (status, review_status) as attributes ready for enum/lookup implementation. Updated relationship descriptions to reference the new key attributes. Retained the complete attribute set and many‑to‑many FAVORITE associative entity while improving schema robustness and readiness for implementation.
